informal: If $\mathcal{T}_\alpha$ is a family of topologies on $X$, show that $\bigcap \mathcal{T}_\alpha$ is a topology on $X$.
lean:
theorem Munkres_exercise_13_4a1 (X I : Type*) (T : I → Set (Set X)) (h : ∀ i, is_topology X (T i)) :
  is_topology X (⋂ i : I, T i)
reasoning: The Lean signature quantifies over an arbitrary index type I and an arbitrary family T : I → Set (Set X) of collections that are each topologies on X (via the hypothesis `∀ i, is_topology X (T i)`), and concludes that the indexed intersection `⋂ i : I, T i` is also a topology on X. This is exactly the informal claim that the intersection of a family of topologies on X is a topology on X. The index type I is allowed to be empty or arbitrary, matching the informal "family ${\mathcal T}_\alpha$". Assuming `is_topology` is the standard predicate, the encoding is logically equivalent to the informal statement.
